body_html:
!Cascade Orienteering Club Logo: Mountains inside a square with a compass rose pointing North, West, East, and South

Bearing 315

The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club | January-February 2000

COC Annual Meeting Report

Everyone seemed to have a great time at COC's annual meeting and dinner.

Jan Urban, Aaron Charlop, Rick Breseman, Dave Tallent, and Kelsey Breseman went home with either a Coco's pie or a club shirt, based on their excellent performances in “Map Segment Match O.” Additionally, all were treated to an enjoyable virtual trip of last summer's World Orienteering Championships in Scotland, courtesy of Ardis Dull. Rounding out the entertainment end of things, the dynamic duo (Dave Tallent harmonizing, with Debbie Newell singing lead and playing guitar) finished up the evening with an especially nice holiday song. Of course, the main official reason for the meeting was to attend to business.

We once again held our famous “2-minute election.” Congratulations to Don Atkinson, Eric Bone, Debbie Newell, Dave Tallent, and Jan Urban for being elected to the COC Board of Directors for 2000. These 5 will elect the club officers and the president will then appoint the coordinators. Current President Dave Tallent recapped 1999 and previewed the coming year. The club also had the pleasure of recognizing 2 outstanding individuals. Aaron Charlop received the 1999 President's Award for outstanding service during the year. And Jim Siscel received the Life Service Award for his continued outstanding contributions over many years.

All in all, it was a good night.
Rick Hood

New Web Address

We now have a new address for our web site: www.CascadeOC.org. Be sure to update your bookmarks to the new address.

The Ultimate Orienteer

This spring, we will begin a new O' series: the Ultimate Orienteer. The series will consist of 4 events, featuring 4 different forms of orienteering, designed to determine The Ultimate Orienteer. At each meet, points will be awarded based on your placing. Your best 3 scores will be added together to determine your final placing in the series. The top man will be crowned The Ultimate Male Orienteer and the top woman will be crowned The Ultimate Female Orienteer. The schedule for the series is:

DateFormatLocation
3/12Short-OMagnuson Park, Seattle
4/1Score-OShoreview Park, Shoreline
4/23Classic OPriest Pt. Park, Olympia
5/13Long-OCle Elum Ridge, Cle Elum

All meets will include courses for beginning and intermediate orienteers. If you have any questions or comments about the series, please contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net).

block_index.text: !Cascade Orienteering Club Logo: Mountains inside a square with a compass rose pointing North, West, East, and South
The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club | January-February 2000
Everyone seemed to have a great time at COC's annual meeting and dinner.

Jan Urban, Aaron Charlop, Rick Breseman, Dave Tallent, and Kelsey Breseman went home with either a Coco's pie or a club shirt, based on their excellent performances in “Map Segment Match O.” Additionally, all were treated to an enjoyable virtual trip of last summer's World Orienteering Championships in Scotland, courtesy of Ardis Dull. Rounding out the entertainment end of things, the dynamic duo (Dave Tallent harmonizing, with Debbie Newell singing lead and playing guitar) finished up the evening with an especially nice holiday song. Of course, the main official reason for the meeting was to attend to business.

We once again held our famous “2-minute election.” Congratulations to Don Atkinson, Eric Bone, Debbie Newell, Dave Tallent, and Jan Urban for being elected to the COC Board of Directors for 2000. These 5 will elect the club officers and the president will then appoint the coordinators. Current President Dave Tallent recapped 1999 and previewed the coming year. The club also had the pleasure of recognizing 2 outstanding individuals. Aaron Charlop received the 1999 President's Award for outstanding service during the year. And Jim Siscel received the Life Service Award for his continued outstanding contributions over many years.
All in all, it was a good night. Rick Hood

We now have a new address for our web site: www.CascadeOC.org. Be sure to update your bookmarks to the new address.

This spring, we will begin a new O' series: the Ultimate Orienteer. The series will consist of 4 events, featuring 4 different forms of orienteering, designed to determine The Ultimate Orienteer. At each meet, points will be awarded based on your placing. Your best 3 scores will be added together to determine your final placing in the series. The top man will be crowned The Ultimate Male Orienteer and the top woman will be crowned The Ultimate Female Orienteer. The schedule for the series is:
All meets will include courses for beginning and intermediate orienteers. If you have any questions or comments about the series, please contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net).

WIOL 5, WINTER O' SERIES 5 - Lake Sammamish State Park, Issaquah. I-90 exit 15. North on 17th Ave NW towards park. Left onto NW Sammamish Rd. Watch for signs after about 0.4 miles.
WIOL 6, WINTER O' SERIES 6 - Fort Steilacoom Park, Tacoma, WA. I-5 exit 129 (72nd W), right onto 72nd.. Becomes Custer, right onto 88th SW, follow signs to start.
WIOL 7, WINTER O' SERIES 7 - Lincoln Park, West Seattle. Exit from I-5 at Spokane St. West to Fauntleroy Way, south to the park.
WIOL CHAMPS, WINTER O' SERIES CHAMPS - Hamlin Park, Shoreline. Exit I-5 at NE 145th. Travel east to 15th Ave NE. Turn north to NE 160th St.
